params.name: The Blue Danube
params.description:
On Mar 9, 1866 AD, Johann Strauss Jr. created this famous waltz.
The Blue Danube is considered the crown jewel of the waltz genre and is a representative work of the "Waltz King" Johann Strauss Jr. It is written in the classic Viennese waltz structure, with an introduction, five small waltzes, and a final section. The composition was born during times of war, as Austria, the composer's homeland, suffered a defeat. At the request of a friend, he created this choral work symbolizing the vitalit …
